visa_types.documents_required:
- Bank Statements provided must be stamped or accompanied by a verification letter from the bank.
- Visa application form (DHA-1738 Form 8), duly completed,
                                          signed and dated
. The application form must be filled in black ink and
                                        signed originally (copies are not acceptable for submission).
- Original Passport.
- 2 Passport sized photographs; See guidelines
                                          on Passport & ID Photograph Specifications (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/28pBanzKTtD5KXBcfMm1On/e79df27ee0cdd19b019464fb2f0dc39a/photo-requirements.pdf)
- Valid passport or travel document valid for 30 days
                                        after the end of the visit to South Africa. Passport must have at least three
                                        (3) blank pages for endorsement purposes, and issued not more than 10 years ago
                                        on the date of submission of your visa application. Copy of main/bio page of
                                        current passport.
- Curriculum Vitae (CV) of the applicant.
- A
medical report. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ZcfV9KMtprFqt6XT6simM/96b61c1275edaee3f7b1ef1ae74db9aa/medical-report.pdf)
– Original
- Cover letter.
- Original Acro UK Police clearance certificates is required
                                        if the applicants are 18 years and older, of all countries where person resided
                                        one year or longer since having attained the age of 18 years and older. (the
                                        police certificate should not be older than 6 months). If applicable.
- All other countries, where person resided for one year or longer since having attained the age of 18 must provide Original police clearance certificate from the specific countries. If an applicant holding a different country Passport and have a UK PR residence or Work Visa must provide Original police clearance certificate from the Passport holder country, If the police clearance is not in English, it must be translated in English and both the certificates has to be Original and not older than 6 months.
- Provisional flight tickets with name of the
                                        applicant. These will only be required if the applicant is spending less than 6
                                        months in RSA
- Proof of accommodation in South Africa. If the
                                        company is providing the accommodation than must get a signed letter confirming
                                        the Staying Along with certified copy of the South African Passport or South
                                        African a Residence Permit in RSA Certified copy of the person representing RSA
                                        institution.
- Critical Skills List , see directive (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/40kg8WYZYFON0m56J3i4rO/85e58122494393aff6580ddb806c6cf3/Immigration-Directive-No-01-of-2022-Critical-Skills-Implementation.pdf)
- Professional Accreditation Bodies and Councils, see directive (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7dMdfRgFfbCLAXsWFbG1rC/6a09973d3c97c62e93a37bc6271dcd72/immigration_critical_skills_160416.pdf)
- Qualification to apply critical Skill Visa
                                            Section 19(4) Regulation 18(1) and 18 (5) and
Critical Skill List (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2ogHYxUmBoey4BL7Uxl2Td/019e66785b427e92ac2e4dc80f509cc8/2022-CRITICAL-SKILLS-LIST.pdf)
- A confirmation, in writing, from the professional body, council or board recognised by SAQA in terms of section 13(1)(i) of the National Qualifications Framework
- Act, or any relevant government Department confirming
                                        the skills or qualifications of the applicant and appropriate post qualification
                                        experience;
- If required by law, proof of application for a certificate of registration with the professional body, council or board recognised by SAQA in terms of section 13(1)(i) of the National Qualifications Framework Act; and
- Proof of evaluation of the foreign qualification by SAQA and translated by a sworn translator into one of the official languages of the Republic.
- A written undertaking by the employer accepting
                                        responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant and his
                                        or her dependent family members, should it become necessary (where there is no
                                        employer: a written undertaking by the applicant must be provided)
- A written undertaking by the employer to ensure that
                                        the passport of his or her employee is valid at all times for the duration of
                                        his or her employment (where there is no employer: a written undertaking by the
                                        applicant to ensure that the passport of the applicant shall be valid at all
                                        times for the duration of his or her temporary visa)
- Signed letter of invitation from the host in RSA with
                                        a certified copy of the ID or RSA Passport. If not South African certified
                                        copies of Residence Permit in RSA the person representing RSA institution not
                                        older than 3 months on the submission date must be submitted.
- A critical skills work visa shall be issued for a
                                        period not exceeding 5 years.
- A spouse and dependent children of a holder of a
                                        critical skills work visa shall be issued with an appropriate visa valid for a
                                        period not exceeding the period of validity of the applicant's critical skills
                                        work visa.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration Regulations.
- For ALL applicants 18 years and over applying under a visitor’s visa, volunteer visa and accompanying dependents:
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements, if applicable, will also render the application incomplete (For an application to be considered complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes copies of bank statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies (where applicable) and medical reports (where applicable) for minors and dependants):
- All applicants must apply in person.
- Fully Confirmed Flight Tickets Required. Provisional flight tickets are required only if the stay is under 12 months.
- We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the, Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request.
- Please note applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Payment can be made by credit card, debit card (except American Express) or cash (change may not be available at the time of the application).
- Only applicants with a UK residential status can apply for a visa.
- Documents issued in a foreign language must be officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
- All application forms filled by hand must be original. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

## Visa Fees
- Visa fees are subject to change annually in April.
- A visa fee of £125.00 will be requested. This must be
                                        paid only at the visa application centre in person and cannot be paid online.
                                        Please see Payment Options below.
- There is an additional service charge of £40.00
                                        (inclusive of VAT) per application in addition to the visa fees for all
                                        applicants. This fee must be paid online only and cannot be paid at the visa
                                        application centre. You will be prompted to pay this fee when scheduling your
                                        appointment. Please ensure that you bring the email confirmation of proof of
                                        payment when attending for your appointment.
- Courier service is mandatory for applications
                                        submitted in Manchester and Edinburgh. This can be paid either online or at the
                                        visa application centre.
- Payment Options - credit card, debit card (except
                                        American Express), or cash (change may not be available at the time of the
                                        application). Bank drafts should be made payable to ‘VF Services UK Limited'
- No personal cheques or company cheques will be
                                        accepted.

- Marriage Certificate in respect of a spouse

- Please click on the following link for
                                            legalization of UK documents –
http://www.fco.gov.uk/en/about-us/what-we-do/services-we-deliver/legal-services/legalisation (http://www.fco.gov.uk/en/about-us/what-we-do/services-we-deliver/legal-services/legalisation)
- Identity Document
- Passport
- Divorce decree
- A 
medical report (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ZcfV9KMtprFqt6XT6simM/96b61c1275edaee3f7b1ef1ae74db9aa/medical-report.pdf)
(Mandatory for all applicants)
- Passport Needs to be submitted For All Long terms Applications
- Police clearance certificates (Original document
                                        required) in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in respect of all
                                        countries where person resided one year or longer since having attained the age
                                        of 18
that all medical reports, radiological
                                        reports and police clearance certificates must be dated and issued no older than
                                        six months at the time of application. Outdated documents will strictly not be
                                        accepted.
Please note that all medical reports and police clearance certificates must be dated and issued no older than six months at the time of application. Outdated documents will strictly not be accepted.
For those applying for a critical skills visa only -
                                        
- SAQA Evaluation Certificate (if you wish to
                                            retain the original)
- Professional body certificate (if you wish to
                                            retain the original)
Please note that the
                                      Embassy of South Africa is only able to certify official South African documents.

- An applicant for a medical treatment visa must also
                                        submit-
- A letter from his or her registered medical
                                            practitioner or medical institution within the Republic confirming
- That space is available at the medical
                                                institution;
- The estimated costs of the treatment;
- Whether or not the disease or ailment is
                                                treatable or curable;
- The treatment schedule; and
- The period of intended treatment in the
                                                Republic;
- The details of, and confirmation by, the person
                                            or institution responsible for the medical expenses and hospital fees:
                                            Provided that in a case where the applicant's medical scheme or employer is
                                            not liable for expenses incurred, proof of financial means to cover the
                                            medical costs shall be submitted;
- The particulars of persons accompanying the
                                            applicant;
- Valid return air flight tickets, where
                                            applicable; and
- Proof of sufficient financial means or provision
                                            for the costs indirectly related to the treatment, in the form of bank
                                            statements (last 3 months, not older than 7 days, stamped or with
                                            verification letter from the bank).

Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner
                                        (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no
                                        longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration Regulations.
- For ALL applicants 18 years and over applying under a visitor’s visa, volunteer visa and accompanying dependents:
- The bank statements must be provided in the
                                            applicant’s name and must be sufficient to cover the full duration of their
                                            visit / volunteer period in South Africa. The requirement is £600 per month,
                                            per person for all short stay visa applications and R8500.00 per month per
                                            person for long stay visa applications- accompanying dependents and R3000.00
                                            per month, per person for Volunteer visas.
- If the amount is being transferred to the
                                            applicant’s account from the parent’s / spouse account, a letter must be
                                            written by the applicant stating that the funds transferred will be used for
                                            the sole purpose of their visit in South Africa.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete (For an application to be considered complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes copies of bank statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies (where applicable) and medical reports (where applicable) for minors and dependants) :

                                        the age of 18 years and older. If applicable

                                            year or longer since having attained the age of 18 must provide Original
                                            police clearance certificate from the specific countries which is not older
                                            than 6 months.

                                            Passport and have a UK PR residence or Work Visa must provide Original

                                            6 months).

                                            must be translated in English and both the certificates has to be Original
                                            and not older than 6 months.
Note: Please note that all medical reports and police clearance certificates must be dated and issued no older than six months at the time of application. Outdated documents will strictly not be accepted.

## GENERAL REQUIREMENTS
- Passport
- Visa application form (DHA-1738 Form 8), duly signed and
                                          dated
. The application form must be filled in black ink and signed
                                        originally (copies are not acceptable for submission).
- 2 Passport sized photographs; See
guidelines
                                          on Passport & ID Photograph Specifications
- Valid passport or travel document valid for 30 days
                                        after the end of the visit to South Africa. Passport must have at least three
                                        (3) blank pages for endorsement purposes and issued not more than 10 years ago
                                        on the date of submission of your visa application.
- A medical report.
- Passport Needs to be submitted For All Long terms Applications
- Police clearance certificates (Original document) in
                                        respect of applicants 18 years and older, in respect of all countries where
                                        person resided one year or longer since having attained the age of 18.
- Marriage certificate or in the case of a foreign
                                        spousal relationship, proof of official recognition thereof issued by the
                                        authorities of the country concerned, if available.
- The affidavit where a spousal relationship to a South
                                        African citizen or resident is applicable, as well as documentation proving
                                        cohabitation and the extent to which the related financial responsibilities are
                                        shared by the parties and setting out the particulars of children in the spousal
                                        relationship.
Please see affidavit in respect of heterosexual and
                                          homosexual relationships
- Divorce decree, where applicable.
- A yellow fever vaccination certificate required, if
                                        the applicant has been in any other country affected by yellow fever in the past
                                        10 years,

## SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS
- In addition, an applicant for a general work visa must submit:
- A written undertaking by the employer accepting
                                            responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant and
                                            his or her dependent family members, should it become necessary; A written
                                            undertaking that the Passport of his or her employee will be valid at all
                                            times for the duration of his or her employment. A certificate from the
                                            Department of Labour confirming that-
- Despite a diligent search, the prospective
                                                employer has been unable to find a suitable citizen or permanent
                                                resident with qualifications or skills and experience equivalent to
                                                those of the applicant;
- The applicant has qualifications or proven
                                                skills and experience in line with the job offer;
- The salary and benefits of the applicant are
                                                not inferior to the average salary and benefits of citizens or permanent
                                                residents occupying similar positions in the Republic; and
- The contract of employment stipulating the
                                                conditions of employment and signed by both the employer and the
                                                applicant is in line with the labour standards in the Republic and is
                                                made conditional upon the general work visa being approved;
- Proof of qualifications evaluated by SAQA and
                                            translated by a sworn translator into one of the official languages of the
                                            Republic;
- Full particulars of the employer, including,
                                            where applicable, proof of registration of the business with the Commission
                                            on Intellectual Property and Companies (CIPC);
- An undertaking by the employer to inform the
                                            Director-General should the applicant not comply with the provisions of the
                                            Act or conditions of the visa; and
- An undertaking by the employer to inform the
                                            Director-General upon the employee no longer being in the employ of such
                                            employer or when he or she is employed in a different capacity or role.
Applicable for all the above: Signed letter of invitation from the host in
                                      RSA with a certified copy of the ID or RSA Passport. If not South African,
                                      certified copies of main page of the Passport + Residence Permit in RSA Certified
                                      copy of the person representing RSA institution not older than 3 months on the
                                      submission date must be submitted.

Please refer: http://www.southafricahouseuk.com/documents/photo_spes.pdf (https://www.sahc.org.au/pdf/dha/Passport_Photo_Requirements.pdf)

                                        periodical list of yellow fever countries).
- The Director-General may issue to the corporate
                                        applicant-
- A corporate visa for a period not exceeding
                                            three years on Form 14 illustrated in Annexure A; and
- Authorisation certificates to employ corporate
                                            workers, in terms of the corporate visa contemplated in paragraph (a), for a
                                            period not exceeding the validity period of the corporate visa.

## SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS
- An application for a corporate worker certificate must be accompanied by:
- A valid Passport of the applicant; The
                                            certificate contemplated in sub regulation (8)(b);
- A valid employment contract;
- A written undertaking by the corporate applicant
                                            to ensure that the foreigner departs from the Republic upon termination of
                                            his or her contract of employment or accepting responsibility for the return
                                            or costs related to the deportation of the foreigner should it become
                                            necessary;
- The documentation contemplated in regulation
                                            9(1)(b), (c) and (f);
- Proof of qualifications evaluated by SAQA, and
                                            translated by a sworn translator into one of the official languages of the
                                            Republic, or skills and experience in line with the job offer; and
- A certificate of registration with the
                                            professional body, council or board recognised by SAQA in terms of section
                                            13(1) (i) of the National Qualifications Framework Act.
- The Director-General may issue to the corporate
                                        worker employed by the holder of a corporate visa contemplated in sub regulation
                                        (8) a corporate worker certificate for a period not exceeding the validity
                                        period of the corporate visa.
- A corporate worker may not renew his or her
                                        corporate worker certificate or apply for a change of status in the Republic.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer: http://www.southafricahouseuk.com/documents/photo_spes.pdf (https://www.sahc.org.au/pdf/dha/Passport_Photo_Requirements.pdf) 

## Processing Time
- Processing time:
- London, Manchester and Edinburgh: Minimum 60 working days
- Applicants should also note that processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application forms below, ensure they are signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment.
Please go to page 150 of the Government Gazette, complete the form, print pages 150, 151 and 152 and bring with you when attending for your appointment. 
Alternately, a hard copy of the form can also be provided when attending the Visa Application Centre’

- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR

- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
PASSPORT HOLDERS WHO ARE EXEMPT FROM
                                  VISAS FOR SOUTH AFRICA
IMMIGRATION DIRECTIVE (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4avfSupwesdVRh47sbztlT/9640085ca62d871384aa3bf86e890287/directive.pdf)
SUBJECT TO CHANGE WITHOUT NOTICE
The citizen who is a holder of a national passport
                                (diplomatic, official and ordinary) of the foreign countries / territories /
                                international organisations listed below are not required to hold a visa when reporting
                                to an immigration officer for an examination at a South African port of entry, subject
                                to the terms and conditions set out in this list, including inter alia the intended
                                period of stay in the Republic.
- The holder of a national South African passport, travel
                                  document and document for travel purposes.
- The citizen who is a holder of a national passport (diplomatic, official or ordinary) of the following countries / territories / international organisations is not required to hold a visa in respect of purposes for which a port of entry visa may be issued or by virtue of being a person contemplated in section 31(3)(b) [accredited in SA] for an intended stay of 90 days or less and when in transit:
- The citizen who is a holder of a national passport
                                  (diplomatic, official and ordinary) of the following countries / territories /
                                  international organisations is not required to hold a visa in respect of purposes for
                                  which a port of entry visa may be issued or by virtue of being a person contemplated
                                  in section 31(3)(b) [accredited in SA] for an intended stay of 30 days or less and
                                  when in transit:
- Agreements have also been concluded with the following
                                  countries for holders of diplomatic and official passport holders.
Click here for list of countries:- (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5lXj5rwn4WKI621U7Kgq0w/b53a7557c90870460ac008c7e90f0b16/passport-holders-who-are-exempt-from-visas-for-zaf.pdf)
- Notwithstanding this Schedule, a foreigner whose visa
                                  exemption has been withdrawn shall comply with the visa requirements until notified by
                                  the Department that his or her visa exemption has been re-instated by the Department
                                  on petition or of its own accord.
- General Work Visas are not required by commercial heavy
                                  duty vehicle drivers who are employed by South African companies and originate from
                                  countries which enjoy visa free movement into South Africa, on condition that they can
                                  produce a letter confirming their employment on entry. In accordance with a High Court
                                  ruling by the North Gauteng High Court on 31 March 2011, foreign commercial heavy duty
                                  vehicle drivers may be issued with visitor’s visas in terms of section 11(1)(a) of the
                                  Immigration Act, with authorisation to conduct work in terms of section 11(2) of the
                                  said Act for the period of their visa exemption, which period may not exceed 90 days
                                  at a time.
- Port of entry visas are not required by foreign employed
                                  commercial heavy-duty vehicle drivers from Lesotho, Swaziland, Botswana, Namibia,
                                  Zambia, Zimbabwe and Malawi who are entering the Republic, provided their visits do
                                  not exceed the period of their visa exemption and on condition that they can produce a
                                  letter confirming their employment with a transport company abroad on entry. Visitor’s
                                  visas in terms of section 11(1)(a) of the Immigration Act, with authorisation to
                                  conduct work in terms of section 11(2) of the said Act for the period of their visa
                                  exemption, which period may not exceed 90 days at a time
- Staff members of the Southern African Development
                                  Community (SADC) who travel on SADC laissez-passers are exempt from visa requirements
                                  for bona fide official business visits up to 90 days and transit.
- The following categories of the UN as well as their
                                  spouses, dependent relatives and other members of the households are exempt from visa
                                  requirements when visiting the Republic for periods not exceeding 90 days for purposes
                                  for which a port of entry visa may be issued, and for official business purposes and
                                  transits and when accredited for placement at a UN mission in the Republic for the
                                  duration of their accreditation, provided they are in possession of the relevant
                                  letters or identification documents to identify themselves at ports of entry as
                                  personnel of an UN agency.
- Holders of United Nations Laissez-passers
- Volunteers attached to the UN
- Persons involved in any United Nations agency
- Persons performing services on behalf of the UN
- Members of military forces attending any military related
                                  matters with the South African National Defence Force are exempt from visa and study
                                  visa requirements; irrespective of their duration of stay provided they are in
                                  possession of letters of invitation from the SANDF as well as letters of consent from
                                  the military force of which they are members.
- With effect from 18 December 2015 travellers, with the
                                  exception of deportees, who transit through O R Tambo International Airport; Cape Town
                                  International Airport; King Shaka International Airport and Lanseria International
                                  Airport do not require transit visas as contemplated in section 10B(4)(a) of the
                                  Immigration Act, 2002 (Act No 13 of 2002). However, transiting passengers will be
                                  subjected to biometric capturing. Deportees transiting at any port of entry are
                                  required to be in possession of a transit visa at all times and must be escorted.
- With effect 16 January 2017, holders of all types of New
                                  Zealand passports (diplomatic, official and ordinary) would need to be in possession
                                  of visas for purposes of visiting the Republic of South Africa with the exception of
                                  those going to transit, please refer to Directive 28 of 2015 or click on transit visas
                                  for more details.

visa_types.documents_required:
- Bank Statements provided must be stamped or accompanied by a verification letter from the bank.
- Visa application form (DHA-84 Form 11),
The application
                                        form must be filled in black ink and signed originally (copies are not
                                        acceptable for submission).
- Original Passport.
- 2 passport sized photographs, See
guidelines
                                          on Passport & ID Photograph Specifications (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/28pBanzKTtD5KXBcfMm1On/e79df27ee0cdd19b019464fb2f0dc39a/photo-requirements.pdf)
- Valid passport or travel document original and copy valid for 30 days
                                        after the end of the visit to South Africa. Passport must have at least three
                                        (3) blank pages for endorsement purposes, and issued not more than 10 years ago
                                        on the date of submission of your visa application.
- Original and copy of current UK Visa or Immigration status along with share code, valid for at least 30 days beyond the return date of the trip
                                        to the South Africa.
- For EU nationals, proof of UK residency or settlement
                                        must be demonstrated follow the link to provide us the UK settlement status.
https://www.gov.uk/check-immigration-status (https://www.gov.uk/check-immigration-status)
. And a Proof
                                        of address copies of council tax bills, utility bills or bank statements
                                        reflecting a UK address to be provided. These documents must be more than 6
                                        months old at the time of application.
- 3 Bank statements with closing balance £600.00 (must be stamped or accompanied by a verification letter from the bank).
- Flight ticket details verified with airline / reservation system.
- Provisional hotel booking covering the whole stay in
                                        RSA will be required with the applicant’s name on.
Invitation Letter from the prospective employer in South Africa and/or United
                                        Kingdom must confirm the following:
- Signed letter of invitation from the host in RSA with
                                        a certified copy of the South African Passport, the certified copy of the
                                        passport should not older than 3 months on the submission date must be
                                        submitted.
- If not South African than a Residence Permit in RSA
                                        Certified copy of the person representing RSA institution, the certified copy of
                                        the ID should be not older than 3 months on the submission date must be
                                        submitted.
- Purpose or necessity of the work;
- Nature of the work;
- Qualification and skills required for the work on the
                                        Company Letter;
- Place and Duration of the work;
- CV of the applicant.
- Proof of remuneration or stipend that the foreigner
                                        will receive from the employer; Identity and contact details of the prospective
                                        employer or relevant contact person from the host institution; along with a
                                        Certified Copy of Host Id in SA, no older than 3 moths and/or their Residence
                                        Permit in RSA. (Certified by solicitor or police station or commissioner of
                                        oaths).
- Proof of accommodation with the applicant’s full name
                                        present on the hotel bookings covering the whole stay in RSA will be required. A
                                        full hotel address must be specified.
- A letter from bona fide organization undertaking to provide proof of accommodation and sufficient financial means; only applicable to film industry
- May be subjected to the relevant individual terms and
                                        conditions, where applicable, be granted subject to a recommendation from any
                                        relevant organisation of state;
- Shall not be extended beyond the validity period of
                                        the visa issued.
- Documents issued in a foreign language must be
                                        officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
- A yellow fever vaccination certificate, if travelling
                                        to or from any other country affected by yellow fever in a space of 14 days
                                        before arriving or aft leaving RSA. This will be based on WHO’s periodical list
                                        of yellow fever countries Please be aware that all decisions regarding the
                                        issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion
                                        of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your
                                        request.
- Where applicable, please submit only certified copies of official documents (Example: Marriage certificates and birth certificates)
- For Minors:
- Certified Copy of Birth Certificate
- Certified Copy of Both Biological Parent's (Main Page)
- Parental Consent Affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths will be required *
- In the absence of one parent is not attending the centre whilst the submission of the application
- If both parents are not attending the centre, and Submitting the application via guardian
- Even if Both parents attend/ and travel with the minor to RSA
- Letter of Financial Support from the parent-sponsoring trip, along with most 90 days' bank statements from the sponsor ( not older than 7 days old)
- All Birth Certificates issued in UK must be legalized
                                        (apostilled) and certified copy of Apostille must be submitted along with
                                        certified copy of the Birth Certificate.
- Letter of financial support from the parent
                                        sponsoring trip, along with the most recent 90 days’ bank statements from the
                                        sponsor.
visa_types.visa_fees:
- Visa fees are subject to change annually in April.
- A visa fee of £35.00 will be requested. This must be
                                        paid only at the visa application centre in person and cannot be paid online.
                                        Please see Payment Options below.
- There is an additional service charge of £40.00
                                        (inclusive of VAT) per application in addition to the visa fees for all
                                        applicants. This fee must be paid online only and cannot be paid at the visa
                                        application centre. You will be prompted to pay this fee when scheduling your
                                        appointment. Please ensure that you bring the email confirmation of proof of
                                        payment when attending for your appointment.
- Courier service is mandatory for applications
                                        submitted in Manchester and Edinburgh. This can be paid either online or at the
                                        visa application centre.
- Payment Options - credit card, debit card (except
                                        American Express), or (change may not be
                                        available at the time of the application). should be made payable to ‘VF
                                        Services UK Limited'.
- No personal cheques or company cheques will be
                                        accepted.

visa_types.processing_time:
- The Processing time of a Short Stay Visa is of a minimum 14 working days.
- Please note VFS Global is not involved in the process of assessment of visa applications, or their grant or refusal. The assessment of visa applications is carried out by the South Africa High Commission, and the grant or refusal of South Africa visas is at their sole discretion.
- The processing times for visas are estimates only. These are based on information and experience of the South Africa High Commission.
- The processing of your application is subject to the procedures and timescales of the South Africa High Commission, over which we have no control.
- Applicants must apply no longer than 5 weeks before their intended date of travel to RSA.
- Applicants should also note that processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Important: Although applicants are entitled to book an appointment at any given time, to ensure your visa is valid for your journey, please select an appointment date no later than 5 weeks prior to your intended date of travel to RSA.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner
                                        (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no
                                        longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration Regulations.
- For ALL applicants 18 years and over applying under a
                                        visitor’s visa, volunteer visa and accompanying dependents:
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete (For an application to be considered complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes copies of bank statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies (where applicable), medical reports (where applicable) and radiological reports (where applicable) for minors and dependants):
- There must be 7 to 10 working days (London) or 12 to
                                        14 working days (Manchester, Edinburgh) before the proposed flight itinerary;
- All applicants must apply in person the forms filled
                                        by hand must be original. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will
                                        strictly not be accepted.
- No fixed flight arrangements should be made until the
                                        visa has been attained.
- Payment can be made by credit card, debit card
                                        (except American Express) Only applicants with a UK residential status can apply
                                        for a visa.
- Only applicants with a UK residential status can
                                        apply for a visa.
- Documents issued in a foreign language must be
                                        officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
- The authorisation cannot be used in lieu of a work
                                        permit for an on-going regular work activity.
- When granted, the authorisation should be for
                                        specified limited time-frame, to work for a specific entity and at a specific
                                        place, for instance, economic, medical, academic, sporting, cultural or social
                                        (e.g. religious) application. The specific entity referred to in paragraph (c)
                                        above must identify itself and be a bona fide corporate body, medical or
                                        academic institution, sports body, social entity or cultural body, and in case
                                        of a sports body, cultural body or event, account must be taken of the bona fide
                                        of the advertised fixtures or performances and furnish proof.
- An application must be made in writing to the
                                        Director-General by the entity for which the applicant will be carrying out
                                        work, making the case for why the person cannot make an application for a work
                                        permit and furthermore provide reasons why the limited work envisaged is urgent
                                        and necessary. 11(2) will not be granted to any given applicant more than twice
                                        in a year.
- Section 11(2) authorisation is not a casual work
                                        permit, but rather a one-off, non-renewable permission that addresses an
                                        immediate short term or urgent need of a limited duration of work activity that
                                        cannot be met by an application for a work permit.
- Applicants that are exempted for Short Visit /
                                        Transit will still need to apply if they wish to obtain an 11(2) visa.
- An application for Section 11(2) will be deemed
                                        invalid if the above information is not made evident. A Section 11(2) will be
                                        granted at the discretion of the Director-General or delegated authority when
                                        considering an application.
- Fully Confirmed Flight Tickets Required.
- The following will not qualify for section 11(2) authorisation;
- Freelance or self-employed persons
- Contract workers
- Project managers
- Exotic dancers
- Casual labour
- Seasonal labour
- Work-seekers

                                          form Here, and take the

                                          note an appointment may be required for this.

Finally, once you have completed the above process,
                                          please bring the
                                          fingerprint form and all other required documents when attending for your
                                          appointment.

                                        INFORMATION
- Once applicants have decided to apply for Permanent
                                        Residence, they should

                                        clearly indicated on application form 18.

                                        choose a category for
                                        prospective applicants. It is ultimately the applicant's choice to make.

                                        to us.

                                            in the Republic
- In respect of a tour leader or host of a tour, a contract of employment
                                            signed by the employer and the applicant and a written undertaking of
                                            financial responsibility for the applicant

                                      applicable) must be authenticated by means of an apostille from the issuing

Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner
                                        (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no
                                        longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration Regulations.
- For ALL applicants 18 years and over applying under a visitor’s visa, volunteer visa and
                                        accompanying dependents:
- The bank statements must be provided in the
                                            applicant’s name and must be sufficient to cover the full duration of their
                                            visit / volunteer period in South Africa. The requirement is £600 per month,
                                            per person for all short stay visa applications and R8500.00 per month per
                                            person for long stay visa applications- accompanying dependents and R3000.00
                                            per month, per person for Volunteer visas.
- If the amount is being transferred to the
                                            applicant’s account from the parent’s / spouse account, a letter must be
                                            written by the applicant stating that the funds transferred will be used for
                                            the sole purpose of their visit in South Africa.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted.
                                        Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will
                                        also render the application incomplete (For an application to be considered
                                        complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete
                                        sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes copies of bank
                                        statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies
                                        (where applicable), medical reports (where applicable) and radiological reports
                                        (where applicable) for minors and dependants) :
- All copies of ID/Passport/Residence permit of
                                            host in South Africa must be certified copies – that is, certified by the
                                            Police in South Africa or a Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must
                                            be signed;
- All bank statements must be stamped by the bank,
                                            or accompanied by a separate letter from the bank verifying the account,
                                            name and address of the client.
- For Minors:
- Certified Copy of Birth Certificate
- Certified Copy of Both Biological Parent's (Main Page)
- Parental Consent Affidavit (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/38xpv5poX7M9Aprm73Ky2d/4c92be90d691f3c2e016fb70cdc82e5f/parents-consent-affidavit.pdf) signed by both biological parents and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths will be required *
- In the absence of one parent is not attending the centre whilst the submission of the application
- If both parents are not attending the centre, and Submitting the application via guardian
- Even if Both parents attend/ and travel with the minor to RSA
- Letter of Financial Support from the parent-sponsoring trip, along with most 90 days' bank statements from the sponsor ( not older than 7 days old)
- All applicants must apply in person.
- Fully Confirmed Flight Tickets Required. Provisional flight tickets are required only if the stay is under 12 months.
- We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Overview, Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
- Please note all
                                        decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa
                                        are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the
                                        embassy will grant your request.
- Please be aware that applicants should be aware of
                                        the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly.
                                        Please also note that all processing times are minimum as stated, and in some
                                        cases may take longer.
- Payment can be made by credit card, debit card
                                        (except American Express) or cash (change may not be available at the time of
                                        the application).
- Only applicants with a UK residential status can
                                        apply for a visa.
- Documents issued in a foreign language must be
                                        officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
- All application forms filled by hand must be
                                        original. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be
                                        accepted.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner
                                        (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no
                                        longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration Regulations.
- For ALL applicants 18 years and over applying under a visitor’s visa, volunteer visa and accompanying dependents:
- The bank statements must be provided in the
                                        applicant’s name and must be sufficient to cover the full duration of their
                                        visit / volunteer period in South Africa. The requirement is £600 per month, per
                                        person for all short stay visa applications and R8500.00 per month per person
                                        for long stay visa applications- accompanying dependents and R3000.00 per month,
                                        per person for Volunteer visas.
- If the amount is being transferred to the applicant’s
                                        account from the parent’s / spouse account, a letter must be written by the
                                        applicant stating that the funds transferred will be used for the sole purpose
                                        of their visit in South Africa.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete (For an application to be considered complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes copies of bank statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies (where applicable) and medical reports (where applicable) for minors and dependants):
- All applicants must apply in person.
- Fully Confirmed Flight Tickets Required. Provisional flight tickets are required only if the stay is under 12 months.
- We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the
                                        issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion
                                        of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your
                                        request.
- Please note applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Payment can be made by credit card, debit card
                                        (except American Express) or cash (change may not be available at the time of
                                        the application).
- Only applicants with a UK residential status can
                                        apply for a visa.
- Documents issued in a foreign language must be
                                        officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
- All application forms filled by hand must be
                                        original. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be
                                        accepted.
- Subject to section 13(1) of the Act-
- Visas issued for studies at a learning
                                            institution, other than a school contemplated in section 1 of the South
                                            African Schools Act, 1996 (Act No. 84 of 1996), shall be issued for the
                                            duration of the course for which the applicant has registered;
- Visas issued for studies at a learning institution,
                                        which is a school contemplated in section 1 of the South African Schools Act,
                                        1996 (Act No. 84 of 1996), shall be valid for the duration of the period of
                                        study: Provided that the study visa issued for studies at a primary school shall
                                        not exceed eight years and for a secondary school shall not exceed six years
- The holder of a study visa at a learning institution
                                        as defined in section 1 of the Higher Education Act, 1997 (Act No. 101 of 1997)
                                        and section 1 of the Further Education and Training Colleges Act, 2006 (Act No.
                                        16 of 2006), may conduct part-time work for a period not exceeding 20 hours per
                                        week.
- A study visa issued in terms of the Act shall
                                        automatically lapse if the holder thereof fails to register with or is
                                        de-registered from the learning institution at any time during the period for
                                        which his or her visa has been issued or if any of the undertakings referred to
                                        in sub regulation (1)(b) are not met.

## Documents Required
- Bank Statements provided must be stamped or accompanied by a verification letter from the bank.
- Visa application form (DHA-1738 Form 8), duly
                                          completed, signed and dated
. The application form must be filled in black
                                        ink and signed originally (copies are not acceptable for submission).
- Original Passport.
- 2 Passport sized photographs; See
guidelines on Passport & ID Photograph
                                          Specifications
- Valid passport or travel document valid for 30 days
                                        after the end of the visit to South Africa. Passport must have at least three
                                        (3) blank pages for endorsement purposes, and issued not more than 10 years ago
                                        on the date of submission of your visa application.
- Copy of main/bio page of current passport.
- Curriculum Vitae (CV) of the applicant.
- A
medical report (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ZcfV9KMtprFqt6XT6simM/96b61c1275edaee3f7b1ef1ae74db9aa/medical-report.pdf)
– Original
- Original UK ACRO Police clearance certificates is required
                                        if the applicants are 18 years and older, of all countries where person resided
                                        one year or longer since having attained the age of 18 years and older. (the
                                        police certificate should not be older than 6 months). If applicable.
- All other countries, where person resided for one
                                            year or longer since having attained the age of 18 must provide Original
                                            police clearance certificate from the specific countries which is not older
                                            than 6 months.
- If an applicant holding a different country
                                            Passport and have a UK PR residence or Work Visa must provide Original
                                            police clearance certificate from the Passport holder country, If the police
                                            clearance is not in English, it must be translated in English and both the
                                            certificates has to be Original and not older than 6 months.
- Provisional flight tickets with name of the
                                        applicant. These will only be required if the applicant is spending less than 6
                                        months in RSA.
- Proof of accommodation in South Africa.
- Cover letter from the applicant. Explaining the trip
                                        and duration
- Bank statements/ transactions for recent three months
                                        (covering full period of 90 days) – up to date, showing at least 8,500 ZAR per
                                        month. If the applicant has Savings account to show sufficient balance, it can
                                        be submitted. The latest transaction shown must not be older than 7 working days
                                        from the submission day.
- An official letter confirming provisional acceptance
                                        or acceptance at that learning institution and the duration of the course;
- An undertaking by the Registrar or Principal of the
                                        learning institution to-
- Provide proof of registration as contemplated in
                                            the relevant legislation within 60 days of registration; or
- In the event of failure to register by the
                                            closing date, provide the Director- General with a notification of failure
                                            to register within 7 days of the closing date of registration;
- Within 30 days of de-registration, notify the
                                            Director-General that the applicant is no longer registered with such
                                            institution; and
- Within 30 days of completion of studies, notify
                                            the Director-General when the applicant has completed his or her studies or
                                            requires to extend such period of study;
- Proof of medical cover renewed annually for the
                                            period of study with a medical scheme registered in terms of the Medical
                                            Schemes Act;
- In the case of a learner under the age of 18
                                        years-
- An unabridged birth certificate
- A copy of his or her identity document, if
                                            applicable;
- The certificate contemplated in sub regulation
                                            (8)(b);
- Proof of physical address and contact number of
                                            the adult person residing in the Republic, who is acting or has accepted to
                                            act as such learner's guardian, including a confirmatory letter from that
                                            guardian; and
- For Minors:
- Certified Copy of Birth Certificate
- Certified Copy of Both Biological Parent's (Main Page)

                                        the age of 18 years and older. If applicable

                                        year or longer since having attained the age of 18 must provide Original police
                                        clearance certificate from the specific countries which is not older than 6
                                        months.

                                        and have a UK PR residence or Work Visa must provide Original police clearance
                                        certificate from the Passport holder country (no older than 6 months).
- If the police clearance is not in English, it must be
                                        translated in English and both the certificates has to be Original and not older
                                        than 6 months.
- Proof of accommodation in South Africa.
- 3 months Bank statements, showing the income of
                                        37,000 Rand per month (not older than 7 days, stamped or with verification
                                        letter from the bank).
- The minimum payment to a foreigner contemplated in
                                        section 20(1) (a) of the Act shall be 37,000 Rand per month, the amount
                                        determined from time to time by the Minister by notice in the Gazette.
- The net worth contemplated in section 20(1) (b) of
                                        the Act shall be a combination of assets realising 37,000 Rand per month, the
                                        amount determined from time to time by the Minister by notice in the Gazette.
- In order to prove this steady monthly income of
                                        37,000 Rand, bank statements alone will not be sufficient. Must provide
                                        additional proof of income – Assets / Pension letters / Investments / Capital
                                        Bonds / salary slips, and a written proof of every claim of income made during
                                        the application will be required. (All the documents should be the recent one
                                        not older than 7 days from the date of your submission. (Pension letter must be
                                        signed).
- Marriage certificate or in a case of foreign spousal
                                        relationship, proof of official recognition thereof issued by the authorities of
                                        the foreign country of the applicant (where applicable).
- Affidavit where a spousal relationship to a South
                                        African citizen or resident is applicable as well as documentation proving
                                        cohabitation and the extent to which the related financial responsibilities are
                                        shared by the parties and setting out the particulars of children in the spousal
                                        relationship.
- Proof of union recognized in terms of the Recognition
                                        of Customary Marriages Act, 1998 (Act No. 12 of 1998), where applicable.
- Legal separation order (Where applicable).
- Police clearance certificates (Original document) in
                                        respect of applicants 18 years and older, in respect of all countries where
                                        person resided one year or longer.
Please note that all medical reports and police
                                      clearance certificates must be dated and issued no older than six months at the
                                      time of application. Outdated documents will strictly not be accepted.

                                        determined from time to time by the Minister by notice in the Gazette.

                                        the Act shall be a combination of assets realising 37,000 Rand per month, the

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner
                                        (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no
                                        longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration Regulations.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete (For an application to be considered complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes copies of bank statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies (where applicable) and medical reports (where applicable) for minors and dependants):
- All applicants must apply in person.
- Fully Confirmed Flight Tickets Required. Provisional flight tickets are required only if the stay is under 12 months.
- We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the
                                        issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion
                                        of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your
                                        request.
- Please note applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Payment can be made by credit card, debit card
                                        (except American Express) or cash (change may not be available at the time of
                                        the application).
- Only applicants with a UK residential status can
                                        apply for a visa.
- Documents issued in a foreign language must be
                                        officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
- All application forms filled by hand must be
                                        original. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be
                                        accepted.

## Visa Fees
- Visa fees are subject to change annually in April.
- A visa fee of £125.00 will be requested. This must be
                                        paid only at the visa application centre in person and cannot be paid online.
                                        Please see Payment Options below.
- There is an additional service charge of £40.00
                                        (inclusive of VAT) per application in addition to the visa fees for all
                                        applicants. This fee must be paid online only and cannot be paid at the visa
                                        application centre. You will be prompted to pay this fee when scheduling your
                                        appointment. Please ensure that you bring the email confirmation of proof of
                                        payment when attending for your appointment.
- Courier service is mandatory for applications
                                        submitted in Manchester and Edinburgh. This can be paid either online or at the
                                        visa application centre.
- Payment Options - credit card, debit card (except
                                        American Express), or cash (change may not be available at the time of the
                                        application). Bank drafts should be made payable to ‘VF Services UK Limited'
- No personal cheques or company cheques will be
                                        accepted.
Premium Services

[Table:  Work Visa: Intra Company Transfer]

## Documents Required
- Bank Statements provided must be stamped or accompanied by a verification letter from the bank.
- Visa application form (DHA-1738 Form 8), duly
                                          completed, signed and dated
. The application form must be filled in black
                                        ink and signed originally (copies are not acceptable for submission).
- Original Passport.
- 2 Passport sized photographs; See 
guidelines on Passport & ID Photograph
                                          Specifications
- Valid passport or travel document valid for 30 days
                                        after the end of the visit to South Africa. Passport must have at least three
                                        (3) blank pages for endorsement purposes, and issued not more than 10 years ago
                                        on the date of submission of your visa application.
- Copy of main/bio page of current passport.
- Curriculum Vitae (CV) of the applicant.
- A 
medical report (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ZcfV9KMtprFqt6XT6simM/96b61c1275edaee3f7b1ef1ae74db9aa/medical-report.pdf)
. – Original
- Original UK ACRO Police clearance certificates is required
                                        if the applicants are 18 years and older, of all countries where person resided
                                        one year or longer since having attained the age of 18 years and older. (the
                                        police certificate should not be older than 6 months). If applicable.
- All other countries, where person resided for one
                                            year or longer since having attained the age of 18 must provide Original
                                            police clearance certificate from the specific countries which is not older
                                            than 6 months.
- If an applicant holding a different country
                                            Passport and have a UK PR residence or Work Visa must provide Original
                                            police clearance certificate from the Passport holder country, If the police
                                            clearance is not in English, it must be translated in English and both the
                                            certificates has to be Original and not older than 6 months.
- Provisional flight tickets with name of the
                                        applicant. These will only be required if the applicant is spending less than 6
                                        months in RSA.
- Proof of accommodation in South Africa.
- Cover letter from the applicant. Explaining the trip
                                        and duration.
- Letter from the organisation confirming intra-company transfer work visa shall also be accompanied by:
- A written undertaking by the employer accepting
                                            responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant and
                                            his or her dependent family members, should it become necessary;
- A written undertaking that the Passport of his or
                                            her employee will be valid at all times for the duration of his or her
                                            employment.
- The foreigner's contract of employment with the
                                            company abroad valid for a period of not less than six months; and a letter
                                            from:
- The company abroad confirming that the foreigner
                                            shall be transferred to a branch, subsidiary or an affiliate of that company
                                            in the Republic; and
- The branch, subsidiary or an affiliate in the
                                            Republic confirming the transfer of the foreigner and specifying the
                                            occupation and capacity in which that foreigner shall be employed.
- In terms of section 19(5) of the Act, the relevant
                                        employer shall ensure that-
- A foreigner is only employed in the specific
                                            position for which the visa has been issued;
- The foreign employee will at all times comply
                                            with the provisions of the Act and conditions of his or her visa and
                                            undertakes to immediately notify the Director-General if the employee
                                            refuses to comply with the provisions of the Act or conditions of the visa;
                                            and
- A plan is developed for the transfer of skills to
                                            a South African citizen or permanent resident.
- An intra-company transfer work visa shall be
                                            issued for a period not exceeding four years and is not renewable.
- The company referred to in sub regulation
                                            (8)(b)(ii) shall undertake to reimburse the Department any costs incurred in
                                            relation to the deportation of the holder of an intra-company transfer work
                                            visa and any of his or her dependent family members.
- Signed letter of invitation from the host in RSA
                                            with a certified copy of the South African Passport, the certified copy of
                                            the passport should not older than 3 months on the submission date must be
                                            submitted.
- If not South African than a Residence Permit in
                                            RSA Certified copy of the person representing RSA institution, the certified
                                            copy of the ID should be not older than 3 months on the submission date must
                                            be submitted.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer: http://www.southafricahouseuk.com/documents/photo_spes.pdf (https://www.sahc.org.au/pdf/dha/Passport_Photo_Requirements.pdf) 

## Processing Time
- Processing time:
- London, Manchester and Edinburgh: Minimum 60 working days
- Applicants should also note that processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application forms below, ensure they are signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment.
DHA 1738 FORM 8 
MEDICAL REPORT BI 811 
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

                                        relationship, i.e. spouses, mother, father, child, brother or sister. In- laws
                                        and grandchildren are not included and do not qualify.
Important – All marriage

                                      apostille from the issuing authority. Certified copies of the same by a solicitor
                                      will not be acceptable: 
The documents required according to Unabridged Birth Certificate showing both

VISA FEE EXEMPT: - Spouse / Life Partner- Minor children (under age)
For Life Partners of SA (not married) citizen they will need to undergo an

                                      them). No need if they have a marriage certificate.
If any of these requirements will not be met at the time of appointment,
                                          application cannot be submitted, must be returned to the applicant for
                                          completion and new appointment must be scheduled.
